front: Work: Okonkwo kills his adopted son Ikemefuna after the Oracle of the Hills and Caves decrees it, despite an elder's warning 'Bear no hand in his death'
Work: Okonkwo is exiled for seven years after his gun accidentally kills Ezeudu's son at Ezeudu's funeral
Work: a fable of Tortoise who grows wings and feasts with the birds, then crashes back to earth
Work: Okonkwo beats his wife during the Week of Peace
Work: Okonkwo hangs himself after killing a British messenger; the Commissioner considers it a paragraph in 'The Pacification of the Primitive Tribes of the Lower Niger'
Work: Christian missionaries build a church in the 'Evil Forest'; twins are left to die in a pot
Work: a woman quips about her husband's 'guns that never shot,' prompting him to fire at her; inaugural entry of the Heinemann African Writers Series
Work: Obi accepts bribes to pay for an abortion for Clara, an osu (outcast); arrested for bribery; sent to England by the Umuofia Progressive Union
Work: title from T.S. Eliot's 'Journey of the Magi'; Obi discovers an ode to his homeland in a book of A.E. Housman poems
Work: Captain Winterbottom ('Breaker-of-Guns') attempts to appoint the priest Ezeulu as Warrant Chief
Work: Ezeulu's son Oduche converts to Christianity and tries to kill the sacred python; villagers abandon Ezeulu after a famine
Work: the corrupt Minister of Culture Chief Nanga is the subject of a coup that anticipated real Nigerian coups
back: A Man of the People (Chinua Achebe)

front: Work: Sam rises to power as dictator of Kangan; opposed by childhood friends Chris Oriko and Ikem Osodi
Work: Jonathan Iwegbu uses his bike as a taxi; his 'egg-rasher' money is stolen; 'Nothing puzzles God'
Work: the schoolmaster Michael Obi puts up a fence across a path connecting a graveyard with a shrine; a woman dies in childbirth
Work: the clerk Julius steps on a sacrificial egg to the smallpox god Kitikpa; his fiancee Janet dies
Work: called Conrad a 'thoroughgoing racist' for depicting Africa as 'the other world, the antithesis of Europe'
work: 'An Image of Africa' and Other Essays

front: Work: describes a 'young fellow from Yonkers' who didn't know that Yonkers had tribal customs
work: 'An Image of Africa' and Other Essays

front: Author: at a 1962 conference, claimed Nigeria's national literature would be written in English (opposing Ngugi)
work: 'An Image of Africa' and Other Essays

front: Author: editor of the Heinemann African Writers Series; wrote the memoir There Was a Country about the Biafran War
Author: the proverb 'until lions produce their own historian, the story will glorify only the hunter' is attributed to this author
